urn:contentItem:6JHV-2T23-S4NP-P04S-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:56ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced net profit of $92.0m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 30% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 16% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 27.6% sequentially from 87.0c in Q4/2025 to $1.11 in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. Average daily volume of 661,092 shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

IDACORP, Inc., a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary Idaho Power Company, focusing on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. The company's core business centers on delivering reliable electric services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Idaho and Oregon, emphasizing cost-effective energy solutions and infrastructure maintenance. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, including wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to rising demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory policies. This growth is further bolstered by investments in grid modernization and new customer connections, such as those supporting data centers and population increases, enabling IDACORP to enhance its market position in a competitive energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 16th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: WEC, 75 words

April 20: IDACORP quarterly dividends steady

IDACORP today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 88.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, June 01. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended April 20 were $3.48.

Press Release article 2 of 5, Source: Company Website, 275 words

February 19: IDACORP, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Results, Initiates 2026 Earnings Guidance

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA) reported fourth quarter 2025 net income attributable to IDACORP of $43.6 million, or $0.78 per diluted share, compared with $37.9 million, or $0.70 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2024. For the full year ended December 31, 2025, IDACORP reported net income attributable to IDACORP of $323.5 million, or $5.90 per diluted share, compared with $289.2 million, or $5.50 per diluted share, in 2024.

"IDACORP's earnings in 2025 were at the top-end of the previously increased earnings guidance range. Results benefited from customer growth, rate changes, lower income tax expense, and the use of tax credits under the company's Idaho regulatory mechanism," said IDACORP President and Chief Executive Officer Lisa Grow. "Partially offsetting those benefits were higher depreciation and financing costs from infrastructure investments."

"We accomplished a great deal in 2025, from breaking ground on our Boardman-to-Hemingway transmission line to achieving record system reliability for our customers. As we move ahead, we are heavily focused on affordability and reliability for our customers as we continue to execute on our infrastructure development plans, with 2026 being another year of substantial capital investment for our company," Grow added.

IDACORP is initiating its full-year 2026 earnings guidance in the range of $6.25 to $6.45 per diluted share with the expectation that Idaho Power will use less than $30 million of additional tax credits available under the Idaho regulatory mechanism in 2026. The earnings guidance assumes normal weather conditions and power supply expenses throughout the year.

Source: Company Website

Press Release article 3 of 5, Source: Company Website, 301 words

February 05: Events Calendar: IDACORP Schedules Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release & Conference Call

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE:IDA) will report its fourth quarter results on Thursday, February 19, in a news release before the stock markets open. The company will hold an analyst conference call that day at 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time (4:30 p.m. Eastern Time) to discuss the fourth quarter 2025 earnings.

All parties interested in listening may do so through a live Webcast or by calling 855‑761‑5600 for listen-only mode. The passcode for the call is 9290150. The conference call logistics are posted on the company's website (www.idacorpinc.com) and will be included in the company's earnings news release. Slides will be included during the conference call. To access the slide deck, please visit www.idacorpinc.com/investor-relations. A replay of the conference call will be available on the company's website for a period of 12 months and will be available shortly after the call.

Background Information

IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA), Boise, Idaho-based and formed in 1998, is a holding company comprised of Idaho Power, a regulated electric utility; IDACORP Financial, an investor in affordable housing and other real estate tax credit investments; and Ida-West Energy, an operator of small hydroelectric generation projects that satisfy the requirements of the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978. Idaho Power, headquartered in vibrant and fast-growing Boise, Idaho, has been a locally operated energy company since 1916. Today, it serves a 24,000-square-mile service area in Idaho and Oregon. With 17 low-cost hydropower projects at the core of its diverse energy mix, Idaho Power's residential, business, and agricultural customers pay among the nation's lowest prices for electricity. Its 2,100 employees proudly serve more than 660,000 customers with a culture of safety first, integrity always, and respect for all.

Press Release article 4 of 5, Source: WEC, 75 words

January 19: IDACORP quarterly dividends steady

IDACORP today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.0c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 88.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, February 05, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, February 05, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, March 02. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended January 19 were $3.46.

Press Release article 5 of 5, 304 words

December 31 2025: IDACORP: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

In this MD&A section of this report, the general financial condition and results of operations for IDACORP and its subsidiaries and Idaho Power and its subsidiary are discussed. The discussion of IDACORP's and Idaho Power's general financial condition and results of operations for 2024 compared with 2023 can be found in their Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024. See Part II - Item 7 - MD&A in that report for further information on the companies' prior period results of operations. While reading this MD&A, please refer to the accompanying consolidated financial statements of IDACORP and Idaho Power. Also refer to "Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements" and Part I - Item 1A - "Risk Factors" in this report for important information regarding forward-looking statements made in this MD&A section and elsewhere in this report.

INTRODUCTION

IDACORP is a holding company whose principal operating subsidiary is Idaho Power. IDACORP's common stock is listed and trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the trading symbol "IDA". Idaho Power is an electric utility whose rates and other matters are regulated by the IPUC, OPUC, and FERC. Idaho Power generates revenues and cash flows primarily from the sale and distribution of electricity to customers in its Idaho and Oregon service areas, as well as from the wholesale sale and transmission of electricity. On February 13, 2026, Idaho Power entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Oregon electric distribution business and associated distribution assets, as well as certain Oregon transmission assets, to OTEC. The closing of the transaction is subject to various conditions, including approvals of the OPUC, IPUC, and FERC.

3.2 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 275 words

Apr 13, 2026: Oregon Appeals Court Reverses PacifiCorp Wildfire Verdict, Directly Benefiting IDACORP Given Service Territory Overlap and Shared Regulatory Environment

On April 8, 2026, the Oregon Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Berkshire Hathaway Energy's PacifiCorp subsidiary in the James class-action case tied to Oregon's 2020 Labor Day wildfires. A three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The ruling reverses and remands the case back to the trial court.

According to financial estimates, Berkshire has projected the cost could reach tens of billions of dollars, including $1.1 billion awarded in a series of already-completed trials. The decision could pause more than 160 damages trials scheduled through 2027. Separately, PacifiCorp has agreed to pay over $2 billion to settle claims, including $575 million to the federal government. Plaintiffs' counsel has called the ruling a procedural setback and may appeal to the Oregon Supreme Court.

Impact on IDACORP: IDA

Following this news, IDACORP faces a directly favorable development given its geographic and operational proximity to PacifiCorp. IDACORP's subsidiary, Idaho Power, serves customers in southern Idaho and eastern Oregon, regions that overlap with PacifiCorp's service territory and share similar wildfire risk profiles driven by dry climate conditions, mountainous terrain, and extensive power line infrastructure in fire-prone areas.

The Oregon appellate ruling's procedural standard is immediately applicable to any future wildfire litigation against Idaho Power in Oregon courts. Additionally, IDACORP has been expanding its wildfire mitigation investments, and the ruling validates the approach of defending individual claims on their specific facts rather than facing broad class-wide liability.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 02 2026: IDACORP, Inc. Reports Earnings Growth Fueled by Industrial Demand

IDACORP, Inc. has announced a robust performance trajectory, driven by a 2.3% increase in its total customer base and a notable 5.7% surge in industrial energy sales. As of 6th May 2026, this growth is primarily attributed to the expansion of significant projects involving leading tech companies Micron and Meta.

The company is currently operating under a "growth-pays-for-growth" regulatory framework in Idaho. This model is designed to facilitate essential infrastructure improvements while ensuring that existing customers do not bear additional costs during this period of notable expansion. New revenue streams from large-load industrial contracts and the Idaho earnings support mechanism are effectively offsetting the impacts of higher interest rates, depreciation, and costs associated with wildfire mitigation.

Management at IDACORP, Inc. has strategically structured contracts for industrial projects to safeguard shareholders from financial risks. These agreements now encompass customized pricing, upfront payments, and take-or-pay provisions. Despite the high capital intensity of ongoing projects, the company remains committed to operational affordability, maintaining rates 20% to 30% below the national average. Additionally, systems flexibility will be enhanced through the transition of the Valmy Unit 2 from coal to natural gas, which is set to be completed before the upcoming summer peak period.

IDACORP, Inc. has issued earnings guidance for the full year 2026, projecting a range of $6.25 to $6.45 per share. This forecast takes into account typical weather patterns and anticipates less than $30 million in additional tax credit amortization. Looking ahead to 2028, the company expects to commission three major new transmission lines aimed at providing access to diverse energy markets, which will generate significant wheeling revenue for the utility provider.

Furthermore, management plans to update its load growth forecast in late 2024, which is expected to show an upward trend from the current 8.3% Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) growth rate, reflecting increasing industrial demand. The Idaho Commission has also approved the 2026 wildfire mitigation plan, providing necessary regulatory clarity for future spending on fire prevention. With new legislation formalizing the contract process for large-load customers, IDACORP, Inc. is poised to navigate its growth strategy while maintaining affordability for its customers.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

IDACORP, Inc., a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary Idaho Power Company, focusing on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. The company's core business centers on delivering reliable electric services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Idaho and Oregon, emphasizing cost-effective energy solutions and infrastructure maintenance. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, including wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to rising demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory policies. This growth is further bolstered by investments in grid modernization and new customer connections, such as those supporting data centers and population increases, enabling IDACORP to enhance its market position in a competitive energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 16th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: WEC, 75 words

April 20: IDACORP quarterly dividends steady

IDACORP today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 88.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, June 01. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended April 20 were $3.48.

Press Release article 2 of 5, Source: Company Website, 275 words

February 19: IDACORP, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Results, Initiates 2026 Earnings Guidance

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA) reported fourth quarter 2025 net income attributable to IDACORP of $43.6 million, or $0.78 per diluted share, compared with $37.9 million, or $0.70 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2024. For the full year ended December 31, 2025, IDACORP reported net income attributable to IDACORP of $323.5 million, or $5.90 per diluted share, compared with $289.2 million, or $5.50 per diluted share, in 2024.

"IDACORP's earnings in 2025 were at the top-end of the previously increased earnings guidance range. Results benefited from customer growth, rate changes, lower income tax expense, and the use of tax credits under the company's Idaho regulatory mechanism," said IDACORP President and Chief Executive Officer Lisa Grow. "Partially offsetting those benefits were higher depreciation and financing costs from infrastructure investments."

"We accomplished a great deal in 2025, from breaking ground on our Boardman-to-Hemingway transmission line to achieving record system reliability for our customers. As we move ahead, we are heavily focused on affordability and reliability for our customers as we continue to execute on our infrastructure development plans, with 2026 being another year of substantial capital investment for our company," Grow added.

IDACORP is initiating its full-year 2026 earnings guidance in the range of $6.25 to $6.45 per diluted share with the expectation that Idaho Power will use less than $30 million of additional tax credits available under the Idaho regulatory mechanism in 2026. The earnings guidance assumes normal weather conditions and power supply expenses throughout the year.

Source: Company Website

Press Release article 3 of 5, Source: Company Website, 301 words

February 05: Events Calendar: IDACORP Schedules Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release & Conference Call

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE:IDA) will report its fourth quarter results on Thursday, February 19, in a news release before the stock markets open. The company will hold an analyst conference call that day at 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time (4:30 p.m. Eastern Time) to discuss the fourth quarter 2025 earnings.

All parties interested in listening may do so through a live Webcast or by calling 855‑761‑5600 for listen-only mode. The passcode for the call is 9290150. The conference call logistics are posted on the company's website (www.idacorpinc.com) and will be included in the company's earnings news release. Slides will be included during the conference call. To access the slide deck, please visit www.idacorpinc.com/investor-relations. A replay of the conference call will be available on the company's website for a period of 12 months and will be available shortly after the call.

Background Information

IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA), Boise, Idaho-based and formed in 1998, is a holding company comprised of Idaho Power, a regulated electric utility; IDACORP Financial, an investor in affordable housing and other real estate tax credit investments; and Ida-West Energy, an operator of small hydroelectric generation projects that satisfy the requirements of the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978. Idaho Power, headquartered in vibrant and fast-growing Boise, Idaho, has been a locally operated energy company since 1916. Today, it serves a 24,000-square-mile service area in Idaho and Oregon. With 17 low-cost hydropower projects at the core of its diverse energy mix, Idaho Power's residential, business, and agricultural customers pay among the nation's lowest prices for electricity. Its 2,100 employees proudly serve more than 660,000 customers with a culture of safety first, integrity always, and respect for all.

Press Release article 5 of 5, 304 words

December 31 2025: IDACORP: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

In this MD&A section of this report, the general financial condition and results of operations for IDACORP and its subsidiaries and Idaho Power and its subsidiary are discussed. The discussion of IDACORP's and Idaho Power's general financial condition and results of operations for 2024 compared with 2023 can be found in their Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024. See Part II - Item 7 - MD&A in that report for further information on the companies' prior period results of operations. While reading this MD&A, please refer to the accompanying consolidated financial statements of IDACORP and Idaho Power. Also refer to "Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements" and Part I - Item 1A - "Risk Factors" in this report for important information regarding forward-looking statements made in this MD&A section and elsewhere in this report.

INTRODUCTION

IDACORP is a holding company whose principal operating subsidiary is Idaho Power. IDACORP's common stock is listed and trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the trading symbol "IDA". Idaho Power is an electric utility whose rates and other matters are regulated by the IPUC, OPUC, and FERC. Idaho Power generates revenues and cash flows primarily from the sale and distribution of electricity to customers in its Idaho and Oregon service areas, as well as from the wholesale sale and transmission of electricity. On February 13, 2026, Idaho Power entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Oregon electric distribution business and associated distribution assets, as well as certain Oregon transmission assets, to OTEC. The closing of the transaction is subject to various conditions, including approvals of the OPUC, IPUC, and FERC.

3.2 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 275 words

Apr 13, 2026: Oregon Appeals Court Reverses PacifiCorp Wildfire Verdict, Directly Benefiting IDACORP Given Service Territory Overlap and Shared Regulatory Environment

On April 8, 2026, the Oregon Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Berkshire Hathaway Energy's PacifiCorp subsidiary in the James class-action case tied to Oregon's 2020 Labor Day wildfires. A three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The ruling reverses and remands the case back to the trial court.

According to financial estimates, Berkshire has projected the cost could reach tens of billions of dollars, including $1.1 billion awarded in a series of already-completed trials. The decision could pause more than 160 damages trials scheduled through 2027. Separately, PacifiCorp has agreed to pay over $2 billion to settle claims, including $575 million to the federal government. Plaintiffs' counsel has called the ruling a procedural setback and may appeal to the Oregon Supreme Court.

Impact on IDACORP: IDA

Following this news, IDACORP faces a directly favorable development given its geographic and operational proximity to PacifiCorp. IDACORP's subsidiary, Idaho Power, serves customers in southern Idaho and eastern Oregon, regions that overlap with PacifiCorp's service territory and share similar wildfire risk profiles driven by dry climate conditions, mountainous terrain, and extensive power line infrastructure in fire-prone areas.

The Oregon appellate ruling's procedural standard is immediately applicable to any future wildfire litigation against Idaho Power in Oregon courts. Additionally, IDACORP has been expanding its wildfire mitigation investments, and the ruling validates the approach of defending individual claims on their specific facts rather than facing broad class-wide liability.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHFW-V4WN-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:56ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced net profit of $92.0m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 30% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 16% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 27.6% sequentially from 87.0c in Q4/2025 to $1.11 in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 13.3%, Revenue was down 7.6% and Net Profit was up 16.5%.

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

IDACORP, Inc., a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary Idaho Power Company, focusing on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. The company's core business centers on delivering reliable electric services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Idaho and Oregon, emphasizing cost-effective energy solutions and infrastructure maintenance. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, including wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to rising demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory policies. This growth is further bolstered by investments in grid modernization and new customer connections, such as those supporting data centers and population increases, enabling IDACORP to enhance its market position in a competitive energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 16th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: WEC, 75 words

April 20: IDACORP quarterly dividends steady

IDACORP today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 88.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, June 01. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended April 20 were $3.48.

Press Release article 2 of 5, Source: Company Website, 275 words

February 19: IDACORP, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Results, Initiates 2026 Earnings Guidance

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA) reported fourth quarter 2025 net income attributable to IDACORP of $43.6 million, or $0.78 per diluted share, compared with $37.9 million, or $0.70 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2024. For the full year ended December 31, 2025, IDACORP reported net income attributable to IDACORP of $323.5 million, or $5.90 per diluted share, compared with $289.2 million, or $5.50 per diluted share, in 2024.

"IDACORP's earnings in 2025 were at the top-end of the previously increased earnings guidance range. Results benefited from customer growth, rate changes, lower income tax expense, and the use of tax credits under the company's Idaho regulatory mechanism," said IDACORP President and Chief Executive Officer Lisa Grow. "Partially offsetting those benefits were higher depreciation and financing costs from infrastructure investments."

"We accomplished a great deal in 2025, from breaking ground on our Boardman-to-Hemingway transmission line to achieving record system reliability for our customers. As we move ahead, we are heavily focused on affordability and reliability for our customers as we continue to execute on our infrastructure development plans, with 2026 being another year of substantial capital investment for our company," Grow added.

IDACORP is initiating its full-year 2026 earnings guidance in the range of $6.25 to $6.45 per diluted share with the expectation that Idaho Power will use less than $30 million of additional tax credits available under the Idaho regulatory mechanism in 2026. The earnings guidance assumes normal weather conditions and power supply expenses throughout the year.

Source: Company Website

Press Release article 3 of 5, Source: Company Website, 301 words

February 05: Events Calendar: IDACORP Schedules Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release & Conference Call

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE:IDA) will report its fourth quarter results on Thursday, February 19, in a news release before the stock markets open. The company will hold an analyst conference call that day at 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time (4:30 p.m. Eastern Time) to discuss the fourth quarter 2025 earnings.

All parties interested in listening may do so through a live Webcast or by calling 855‑761‑5600 for listen-only mode. The passcode for the call is 9290150. The conference call logistics are posted on the company's website (www.idacorpinc.com) and will be included in the company's earnings news release. Slides will be included during the conference call. Background Information

IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA), Boise, Idaho-based and formed in 1998, is a holding company comprised of Idaho Power, a regulated electric utility; IDACORP Financial, an investor in affordable housing and other real estate tax credit investments; and Ida-West Energy, an operator of small hydroelectric generation projects that satisfy the requirements of the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978. Idaho Power, headquartered in vibrant and fast-growing Boise, Idaho, has been a locally operated energy company since 1916. Today, it serves a 24,000-square-mile service area in Idaho and Oregon. With 17 low-cost hydropower projects at the core of its diverse energy mix, Idaho Power's residential, business, and agricultural customers pay among the nation's lowest prices for electricity. Its 2,100 employees proudly serve more than 660,000 customers with a culture of safety first, integrity always, and respect for all.

INTRODUCTION

IDACORP is a holding company whose principal operating subsidiary is Idaho Power. IDACORP's common stock is listed and trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the trading symbol "IDA". Idaho Power is an electric utility whose rates and other matters are regulated by the IPUC, OPUC, and FERC. Idaho Power generates revenues and cash flows primarily from the sale and distribution of electricity to customers in its Idaho and Oregon service areas, as well as from the wholesale sale and transmission of electricity. On February 13, 2026, Idaho Power entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Oregon electric distribution business and associated distribution assets, as well as certain Oregon transmission assets, to OTEC. The closing of the transaction is subject to various conditions, including approvals of the OPUC, IPUC, and FERC.

Apr 13, 2026: Oregon Appeals Court Reverses PacifiCorp Wildfire Verdict, Directly Benefiting IDACORP Given Service Territory Overlap and Shared Regulatory Environment

On April 8, 2026, the Oregon Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Berkshire Hathaway Energy's PacifiCorp subsidiary in the James class-action case tied to Oregon's 2020 Labor Day wildfires. A three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The ruling reverses and remands the case back to the trial court.

According to financial estimates, Berkshire has projected the cost could reach tens of billions of dollars, including $1.1 billion awarded in a series of already-completed trials. The decision could pause more than 160 damages trials scheduled through 2027. Separately, PacifiCorp has agreed to pay over $2 billion to settle claims, including $575 million to the federal government. Plaintiffs' counsel has called the ruling a procedural setback and may appeal to the Oregon Supreme Court.

Impact on IDACORP: IDA

Following this news, IDACORP faces a directly favorable development given its geographic and operational proximity to PacifiCorp. IDACORP's subsidiary, Idaho Power, serves customers in southern Idaho and eastern Oregon, regions that overlap with PacifiCorp's service territory and share similar wildfire risk profiles driven by dry climate conditions, mountainous terrain, and extensive power line infrastructure in fire-prone areas.

The Oregon appellate ruling's procedural standard is immediately applicable to any future wildfire litigation against Idaho Power in Oregon courts. Additionally, IDACORP has been expanding its wildfire mitigation investments, and the ruling validates the approach of defending individual claims on their specific facts rather than facing broad class-wide liability.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Portland General Electric Company, a publicly traded utility on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of electric power in Oregon, serving residential, commercial, and industrial customers. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, primarily sourced from a diverse mix including hydroelectric, natural gas, and coal facilities. In recent years, the company's fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy sources, such as wind and solar power, driven by investments in clean energy projects to meet increasing demand for sustainable options and comply with environmental regulations. This expansion supports PGE's efforts to reduce carbon emissions and enhance grid reliability, while also positioning the company for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. Through strategic initiatives in energy efficiency and infrastructure modernization, PGE continues to play a vital role in supporting Oregon's economic development. It is the NYSE's 18th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: WEC, 79 words

February 18: Portland General Electric quarterly dividends steady

Portland General Electric today announced a quarterly dividend of 52.50c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 52.50c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, March 23, 2026 and the record date is Monday, March 23, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended February 18 were $2.60.

November 17 2025: Sealed Air to be Acquired by CD&R for $10.3 Billion

CHARLOTTE, N.C., November 17, 2025 - Sealed Air Corporation ("Sealed Air" or the "Company") (NYSE: SEE), a leading global provider of food and protective packaging solutions, announced today that it has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by funds affiliated with CD&R, a leading private investment firm with deep experience in the industrial and packaging industries, in an all-cash transaction with an enterprise value of $10.3 billion.

Under the terms of the agreement, Sealed Air stockholders will receive $42.15 in cash per share. This represents a premium of 41% to Sealed Air's unaffected stock price as of August 14, 2025 and a premium of 24% to Sealed Air's 90-day VWAP as of November 12, 2025.

"After careful review of strategic alternatives over the past year, the Board is confident that this transaction delivers significant value and is in the best interests of our stockholders and the Company," said Henry R. Keizer, Chairman of the Board of Sealed Air. "Furthermore, this transaction represents a realization of immediate and certain value for Sealed Air stockholders at a substantial premium and enables the Company to execute its long-term strategic vision."

"Today marks an inflection point in Sealed Air's history and we look forward to embarking on this next phase of growth in partnership with CD&R," said Dustin Semach, President and Chief Executive Officer of Sealed Air. "This transaction delivers significant and derisked value to Sealed Air stockholders while accelerating our ongoing transformation. CD&R's partnership will enhance our ability to invest in growing our Food and Protective businesses while maintaining a customer-first approach. Through more rapid innovation, expanded capabilities and broader reach, we will create more value for our customers and more opportunities for our employees."

"Sealed Air is an exceptional global business with a talented leadership team, leading franchises and attractive underlying fundamentals," said Rob Volpe, Partner at CD&R.

November 12 2025: PGE customers can take action now to prepare for stormy winter weather

PORTLAND, Ore. (November 12, 2025) - Portland General Electric plans and prepares year-round for inclement and extreme weather conditions and encourages customers to be prepared, too. As winter approaches, the potential for weather-related power outages caused by strong wind, snow and ice grows each day.

Our goal is to keep the lights on for our customers. That's why we're always working to run the grid safely and reliably. We prepare for storms and extreme weather by trimming trees near power lines, using advanced weather forecasts and monitoring conditions with high-tech tools like our AI-powered cameras and weather stations.

Important safety information

If you see a downed power line:

Never touch a downed power line - or anything else the line is touching.

Always assume powerlines are live and stay at least 50 feet away (that's a little longer than a city bus).

Downed power lines don't have to spark or sizzle to be live, so you should always assume they are electrified and extremely dangerous.

If you see downed power lines, call 911, then report it to PGE at 503-464-7777.

Things customers can do now to prepare

Prepare an outage kit that includes:

Flashlight or headlamps

Battery-powered or hand crank radio and alarm clock or watch

Car charger for your cell phone, laptop or tablet

72-hour supply of ready-to-eat food and water

Any required medications

Extra blankets

Bottled water for people and animals

Be familiar with safe cooking, heating and lighting practices. Information on safe generator use and other safety tips are available on portlandgeneral.com/safety.

If you have an electric garage door opener, learn how to operate it manually.

For more winter-ready tips, visit portlandgeneral.com/beprepared.

What to do if your power goes out. For customers whose power is out, PGE offers several ways to get updates or report an outage:

Text updates: Please make sure PGE has your contact information up to date so we can provide you with outage information via text message (outage texts are currently available for residential and small business customers with fewer than six accounts).

Online: Go to portlandgeneral.com/outage to view PGE's outage map, which is the best place to go for real-time outage information.

App: Download the PGE app at the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

November 07 2025: Portland General Electric introduces temporary protections for income-qualified and medical certificate customers amid government shutdown

To support customers affected by the ongoing U.S. federal government shut down and delays in federal assistance programs, Portland General Electric is temporarily expanding protections for customers enrolled in its Income-Qualified Bill Discount and medical certificate programs. Through the end of the year, PGE will suspend service disconnections for Income-Qualified Bill Discount recipients and medical certificate customers.

"PGE understands that some customers are facing difficult decisions," said Allison Rowden, PGE director of customer service. "We are here for our customers through bill assistance programs and options that can help with your specific situation. We're just a phone call away."

These temporary measures are in addition to PGE's regular winter protections, which prohibit disconnections for all residential customers during times of extreme weather. Together, these measures help ensure customers maintain access to safe, reliable service during this period of uncertainty. Approximately 19,000 households throughout PGE's service area have lost access to Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP).

PGE's Income-Qualified Bill Discount Program is not affected by the government shutdown, and customers enrolled in the program will continue to receive their bill discounts. Residential customers not currently enrolled with a household income at or below 60% of Oregon's median family income may be eligible to enroll in the program. The exact size of the discount percentage will vary based on household size and income level.

To enroll, eligible customers can sign up

Online at portlandgeneral.com/iqbd

Call PGE's Customer Service at 503-228-6322, where teams are ready to assist in more than 200 languages.

The Income Qualified Discount Program is just one way that PGE works closely with customers who are struggling to pay their bills.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Portland General Electric Company, a publicly traded utility on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of electric power in Oregon, serving residential, commercial, and industrial customers. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, primarily sourced from a diverse mix including hydroelectric, natural gas, and coal facilities. In recent years, the company's fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy sources, such as wind and solar power, driven by investments in clean energy projects to meet increasing demand for sustainable options and comply with environmental regulations. This expansion supports PGE's efforts to reduce carbon emissions and enhance grid reliability, while also positioning the company for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. Through strategic initiatives in energy efficiency and infrastructure modernization, PGE continues to play a vital role in supporting Oregon's economic development. It is the NYSE's 18th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

February 18: Portland General Electric quarterly dividends steady

Portland General Electric today announced a quarterly dividend of 52.50c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 52.50c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, March 23, 2026 and the record date is Monday, March 23, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended February 18 were $2.60.

November 17 2025: Sealed Air to be Acquired by CD&R for $10.3 Billion

CHARLOTTE, N.C., November 17, 2025 - Sealed Air Corporation ("Sealed Air" or the "Company") (NYSE: SEE), a leading global provider of food and protective packaging solutions, announced today that it has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by funds affiliated with CD&R, a leading private investment firm with deep experience in the industrial and packaging industries, in an all-cash transaction with an enterprise value of $10.3 billion.

Under the terms of the agreement, Sealed Air stockholders will receive $42.15 in cash per share. This represents a premium of 41% to Sealed Air's unaffected stock price as of August 14, 2025 and a premium of 24% to Sealed Air's 90-day VWAP as of November 12, 2025.

"After careful review of strategic alternatives over the past year, the Board is confident that this transaction delivers significant value and is in the best interests of our stockholders and the Company," said Henry R. Keizer, Chairman of the Board of Sealed Air. "Furthermore, this transaction represents a realization of immediate and certain value for Sealed Air stockholders at a substantial premium and enables the Company to execute its long-term strategic vision."

"Today marks an inflection point in Sealed Air's history and we look forward to embarking on this next phase of growth in partnership with CD&R," said Dustin Semach, President and Chief Executive Officer of Sealed Air. "This transaction delivers significant and derisked value to Sealed Air stockholders while accelerating our ongoing transformation. CD&R's partnership will enhance our ability to invest in growing our Food and Protective businesses while maintaining a customer-first approach. Through more rapid innovation, expanded capabilities and broader reach, we will create more value for our customers and more opportunities for our employees."

"Sealed Air is an exceptional global business with a talented leadership team, leading franchises and attractive underlying fundamentals," said Rob Volpe, Partner at CD&R.

November 12 2025: PGE customers can take action now to prepare for stormy winter weather

PORTLAND, Ore. (November 12, 2025) - Portland General Electric plans and prepares year-round for inclement and extreme weather conditions and encourages customers to be prepared, too. As winter approaches, the potential for weather-related power outages caused by strong wind, snow and ice grows each day.

Our goal is to keep the lights on for our customers. That's why we're always working to run the grid safely and reliably. We prepare for storms and extreme weather by trimming trees near power lines, using advanced weather forecasts and monitoring conditions with high-tech tools like our AI-powered cameras and weather stations.

Important safety information

If you see a downed power line:

Never touch a downed power line - or anything else the line is touching.

Always assume powerlines are live and stay at least 50 feet away (that's a little longer than a city bus).

Downed power lines don't have to spark or sizzle to be live, so you should always assume they are electrified and extremely dangerous.

If you see downed power lines, call 911, then report it to PGE at 503-464-7777.

Things customers can do now to prepare

Prepare an outage kit that includes:

Flashlight or headlamps

Battery-powered or hand crank radio and alarm clock or watch

Car charger for your cell phone, laptop or tablet

72-hour supply of ready-to-eat food and water

Any required medications

Extra blankets

Bottled water for people and animals

Be familiar with safe cooking, heating and lighting practices. Information on safe generator use and other safety tips are available on portlandgeneral.com/safety.

If you have an electric garage door opener, learn how to operate it manually.

For more winter-ready tips, visit portlandgeneral.com/beprepared.

What to do if your power goes out. For customers whose power is out, PGE offers several ways to get updates or report an outage:

Text updates: Please make sure PGE has your contact information up to date so we can provide you with outage information via text message (outage texts are currently available for residential and small business customers with fewer than six accounts).

Online: Go to portlandgeneral.com/outage to view PGE's outage map, which is the best place to go for real-time outage information.

App: Download the PGE app at the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

November 07 2025: Portland General Electric submits inaugural Community Benefits & Impacts Advisory Group Biennial Report

At the center of Portland General Electric's Community Benefits & Impacts Advisory Group (CBIAG) is a commitment to inclusion, collaboration and trust. With input from the CBIAG, PGE recently submitted its first-ever Community Benefits & Impacts Advisory Group Biennial Report, which showcases how the group was established, what was accomplished in its first cycle and what future priorities will guide the CBIAG's feedback as PGE continues working toward its clean energy transition.

"This report is about partnership," said Jenn Latu, PGE's manager of equitable engagement. "It shows what happens when utilities listen deeply and act with community, not for community."

When Oregon's House Bill 2021 became law, it required all Oregon investor-owned electric companies that file a clean energy plan to convene a Community Benefits & Impacts Advisory Group. The CBIAG serves as an advisory group representing the geographic diversity of PGE's service area and the interests of environmental justice communities, which includes communities of color, tribal and rural communities and others historically underrepresented in public processes and disproportionately affected by environmental and health hazards. Since its launch in 2023, PGE's CBIAG has provided valuable feedback and insights that have strengthened programs and resources, supported affordability initiatives and enhanced outreach and engagement efforts for customers and communities.

KEY LEARNINGS Affordability and access:

Customer affordability was a central theme in CBIAG discussions, and member feedback led to improvements in PGE's Income-Qualified Bill Discount program. Members encouraged more multilingual and culturally specific outreach, so support reaches customers most affected by high energy usage. Additionally, they provided opportunities for PGE to connect directly with communities they serve by opening their events and trusted spaces for outreach and information sharing about PGE customer programs and resources.

Infrastructure and resilience:

In CBIAG discussions on grid resiliency, members emphasized the importance of including environmental justice communities.

Apr 13, 2026: Oregon Appeals Court Reverses PacifiCorp Wildfire Verdict, Directly Impacting Portland General Electric's Wildfire Litigation Risk as Oregon's Other Major Utility

On April 8, 2026, the Oregon Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Berkshire Hathaway Energy's PacifiCorp subsidiary in the James class-action case tied to Oregon's 2020 Labor Day wildfires. A three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The ruling reverses and remands the case back to the trial court.

According to financial estimates, Berkshire has projected the cost could reach tens of billions of dollars, including $1.1 billion awarded in a series of already-completed trials. The decision could pause more than 160 damages trials scheduled through 2027. Separately, PacifiCorp has agreed to pay over $2 billion to settle claims, including $575 million to the federal government. Plaintiffs' counsel has called the ruling a procedural setback and may appeal to the Oregon Supreme Court.

Impact on Portland General Electric: POR

Following this news, Portland General Electric faces the most directly relevant precedent of any utility peer, given that it operates exclusively in Oregon and is subject to the same state appellate courts that issued the PacifiCorp ruling. PGE serves approximately 930,000 customers in the greater Portland metropolitan area and northwestern Oregon, a region that experienced severe wildfire conditions during the same 2020 Labor Day weekend fires at the center of the PacifiCorp litigation.

The appellate court's procedural holding now applies as binding Oregon precedent for any wildfire class-action litigation involving PGE. Should PGE face future wildfire-related claims, plaintiffs will be unable to use class-wide evidence assumptions across geographically distinct fire events, providing a significant procedural protection for Oregon's second-largest investor-owned utility.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Portland General Electric Company, a publicly traded utility on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of electric power in Oregon, serving residential, commercial, and industrial customers. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, primarily sourced from a diverse mix including hydroelectric, natural gas, and coal facilities. In recent years, the company's fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy sources, such as wind and solar power, driven by investments in clean energy projects to meet increasing demand for sustainable options and comply with environmental regulations. This expansion supports PGE's efforts to reduce carbon emissions and enhance grid reliability, while also positioning the company for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. Through strategic initiatives in energy efficiency and infrastructure modernization, PGE continues to play a vital role in supporting Oregon's economic development. It is the NYSE's 18th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Black Hills Corporation (NYSE:BKH) is a diversified utility company, strategically positioned to capitalize on sustained demand across its electric utility and natural gas utility segments. With its robust growth outlook, Black Hills Corp consistently draws investor interest, particularly surrounding its attractive BKH dividend and notable dividend history. The company recently announced its Black Hills Corp schedules 2026 first-quarter earnings release, which will be elaborated during the Black Hills conference call Q1 2026. Stakeholders monitor the Black Hills dividend declaration closely, particularly in light of the Black Hills ex-dividend date and BKH dividend yield, indicating strong Black Hills investor news dividend traction. The upcoming Black Hills Corp 2026 Q1 earnings release promises insights into Black Hills EPS Q1 2026 trends, Black Hills revenue Q1 2026 metrics, and Black Hills financial results Q1 2026 performance. As Black Hills stock news dividend announcements continue to pivot the utilities sector, the company’s resilient business model underpins its stable Black Hills payout ratio.

February 18: Portland General Electric quarterly dividends steady

Portland General Electric today announced a quarterly dividend of 52.50c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 52.50c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, March 23, 2026 and the record date is Monday, March 23, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended February 18 were $2.60.

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS.

In addition to any assumptions and other factors and matters referred to specifically in connection with forward-looking statements, risks, uncertainties and other important factors that could cause actual results or outcomes for PGE to differ materially from those discussed in such forward-looking statements include:

uncertainties associated with increased energy demand or significant accelerated growth in demand due to new data centers, including the concentration of data centers, and the ability to obtain regulatory approvals, environmental, and other permits to construct new facilities in a timely manner; Press Release article 3 of 5, Source: Company Website, 327 words

November 17 2025: Sealed Air to be Acquired by CD&R for $10.3 Billion

CHARLOTTE, N.C., November 17, 2025 - Sealed Air Corporation ("Sealed Air" or the "Company") (NYSE: SEE), a leading global provider of food and protective packaging solutions, announced today that it has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by funds affiliated with CD&R, a leading private investment firm with deep experience in the industrial and packaging industries, in an all-cash transaction with an enterprise value of $10.3 billion.

Under the terms of the agreement, Sealed Air stockholders will receive $42.15 in cash per share. This represents a premium of 41% to Sealed Air's unaffected stock price as of August 14, 2025 and a premium of 24% to Sealed Air's 90-day VWAP as of November 12, 2025.

"After careful review of strategic alternatives over the past year, the Board is confident that this transaction delivers significant value and is in the best interests of our stockholders and the Company," said Henry R. Keizer, Chairman of the Board of Sealed Air. "Furthermore, this transaction represents a realization of immediate and certain value for Sealed Air stockholders at a substantial premium and enables the Company to execute its long-term strategic vision."

"Today marks an inflection point in Sealed Air's history and we look forward to embarking on this next phase of growth in partnership with CD&R," said Dustin Semach, President and Chief Executive Officer of Sealed Air. "This transaction delivers significant and derisked value to Sealed Air stockholders while accelerating our ongoing transformation. CD&R's partnership will enhance our ability to invest in growing our Food and Protective businesses while maintaining a customer-first approach. Through more rapid innovation, expanded capabilities and broader reach, we will create more value for our customers and more opportunities for our employees."

"Sealed Air is an exceptional global business with a talented leadership team, leading franchises and attractive underlying fundamentals," said Rob Volpe, Partner at CD&R.

Press Release article 4 of 5, Source: Company Website, 376 words

November 12 2025: PGE customers can take action now to prepare for stormy winter weather

PORTLAND, Ore. (November 12, 2025) - Portland General Electric plans and prepares year-round for inclement and extreme weather conditions and encourages customers to be prepared, too. As winter approaches, the potential for weather-related power outages caused by strong wind, snow and ice grows each day.

Our goal is to keep the lights on for our customers. That's why we're always working to run the grid safely and reliably. We prepare for storms and extreme weather by trimming trees near power lines, using advanced weather forecasts and monitoring conditions with high-tech tools like our AI-powered cameras and weather stations.

Important safety information

If you see a downed power line:

Never touch a downed power line - or anything else the line is touching.

Always assume powerlines are live and stay at least 50 feet away (that's a little longer than a city bus).

Downed power lines don't have to spark or sizzle to be live, so you should always assume they are electrified and extremely dangerous.

If you see downed power lines, call 911, then report it to PGE at 503-464-7777.

Things customers can do now to prepare

Prepare an outage kit that includes:

Flashlight or headlamps

Battery-powered or hand crank radio and alarm clock or watch

Car charger for your cell phone, laptop or tablet

72-hour supply of ready-to-eat food and water

Any required medications

Extra blankets

Bottled water for people and animals

Be familiar with safe cooking, heating and lighting practices. Information on safe generator use and other safety tips are available on portlandgeneral.com/safety.

If you have an electric garage door opener, learn how to operate it manually.

For more winter-ready tips, visit portlandgeneral.com/beprepared.

What to do if your power goes out. For customers whose power is out, PGE offers several ways to get updates or report an outage:

Text updates: Please make sure PGE has your contact information up to date so we can provide you with outage information via text message (outage texts are currently available for residential and small business customers with fewer than six accounts).

Online: Go to portlandgeneral.com/outage to view PGE's outage map, which is the best place to go for real-time outage information.

App: Download the PGE app at the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: Company Website, 293 words

November 07 2025: Portland General Electric introduces temporary protections for income-qualified and medical certificate customers amid government shutdown

To support customers affected by the ongoing U.S. federal government shut down and delays in federal assistance programs, Portland General Electric is temporarily expanding protections for customers enrolled in its Income-Qualified Bill Discount and medical certificate programs. Through the end of the year, PGE will suspend service disconnections for Income-Qualified Bill Discount recipients and medical certificate customers.

"PGE understands that some customers are facing difficult decisions," said Allison Rowden, PGE director of customer service. "We are here for our customers through bill assistance programs and options that can help with your specific situation. We're just a phone call away."

These temporary measures are in addition to PGE's regular winter protections, which prohibit disconnections for all residential customers during times of extreme weather. Together, these measures help ensure customers maintain access to safe, reliable service during this period of uncertainty. Approximately 19,000 households throughout PGE's service area have lost access to Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP).

PGE's Income-Qualified Bill Discount Program is not affected by the government shutdown, and customers enrolled in the program will continue to receive their bill discounts. Residential customers not currently enrolled with a household income at or below 60% of Oregon's median family income may be eligible to enroll in the program. The exact size of the discount percentage will vary based on household size and income level.

To enroll, eligible customers can sign up

Online at portlandgeneral.com/iqbd

Call PGE's Customer Service at 503-228-6322, where teams are ready to assist in more than 200 languages.

3.2 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 294 words

Apr 13, 2026: Oregon Appeals Court Reverses PacifiCorp Wildfire Verdict, Directly Impacting Portland General Electric's Wildfire Litigation Risk as Oregon's Other Major Utility

On April 8, 2026, the Oregon Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Berkshire Hathaway Energy's PacifiCorp subsidiary in the James class-action case tied to Oregon's 2020 Labor Day wildfires. A three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The ruling reverses and remands the case back to the trial court.

According to financial estimates, Berkshire has projected the cost could reach tens of billions of dollars, including $1.1 billion awarded in a series of already-completed trials. The decision could pause more than 160 damages trials scheduled through 2027. Separately, PacifiCorp has agreed to pay over $2 billion to settle claims, including $575 million to the federal government. Plaintiffs' counsel has called the ruling a procedural setback and may appeal to the Oregon Supreme Court.

Impact on Portland General Electric: POR

Following this news, Portland General Electric faces the most directly relevant precedent of any utility peer, given that it operates exclusively in Oregon and is subject to the same state appellate courts that issued the PacifiCorp ruling. PGE serves approximately 930,000 customers in the greater Portland metropolitan area and northwestern Oregon, a region that experienced severe wildfire conditions during the same 2020 Labor Day weekend fires at the center of the PacifiCorp litigation.

The appellate court's procedural holding now applies as binding Oregon precedent for any wildfire class-action litigation involving PGE. Should PGE face future wildfire-related claims, plaintiffs will be unable to use class-wide evidence assumptions across geographically distinct fire events, providing a significant procedural protection for Oregon's second-largest investor-owned utility.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

IDACORP, Inc., a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary Idaho Power Company, focusing on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. The company's core business centers on delivering reliable electric services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Idaho and Oregon, emphasizing cost-effective energy solutions and infrastructure maintenance. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, including wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to rising demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory policies. This growth is further bolstered by investments in grid modernization and new customer connections, such as those supporting data centers and population increases, enabling IDACORP to enhance its market position in a competitive energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 16th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: WEC, 75 words

April 20: IDACORP quarterly dividends steady

IDACORP today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 88.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, May 05, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, June 01. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended April 20 were $3.48.

Press Release article 2 of 5, Source: Company Website, 275 words

February 19: IDACORP, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Results, Initiates 2026 Earnings Guidance

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA) reported fourth quarter 2025 net income attributable to IDACORP of $43.6 million, or $0.78 per diluted share, compared with $37.9 million, or $0.70 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2024. For the full year ended December 31, 2025, IDACORP reported net income attributable to IDACORP of $323.5 million, or $5.90 per diluted share, compared with $289.2 million, or $5.50 per diluted share, in 2024.

"IDACORP's earnings in 2025 were at the top-end of the previously increased earnings guidance range. Results benefited from customer growth, rate changes, lower income tax expense, and the use of tax credits under the company's Idaho regulatory mechanism," said IDACORP President and Chief Executive Officer Lisa Grow. "Partially offsetting those benefits were higher depreciation and financing costs from infrastructure investments."

"We accomplished a great deal in 2025, from breaking ground on our Boardman-to-Hemingway transmission line to achieving record system reliability for our customers. As we move ahead, we are heavily focused on affordability and reliability for our customers as we continue to execute on our infrastructure development plans, with 2026 being another year of substantial capital investment for our company," Grow added.

IDACORP is initiating its full-year 2026 earnings guidance in the range of $6.25 to $6.45 per diluted share with the expectation that Idaho Power will use less than $30 million of additional tax credits available under the Idaho regulatory mechanism in 2026. The earnings guidance assumes normal weather conditions and power supply expenses throughout the year.

Source: Company Website

Press Release article 3 of 5, Source: Company Website, 301 words

February 05: Events Calendar: IDACORP Schedules Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release & Conference Call

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE:IDA) will report its fourth quarter results on Thursday, February 19, in a news release before the stock markets open. The company will hold an analyst conference call that day at 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time (4:30 p.m. Eastern Time) to discuss the fourth quarter 2025 earnings.

All parties interested in listening may do so through a live Webcast or by calling 855‑761‑5600 for listen-only mode. The passcode for the call is 9290150. The conference call logistics are posted on the company's website (www.idacorpinc.com) and will be included in the company's earnings news release. Slides will be included during the conference call. To access the slide deck, please visit www.idacorpinc.com/investor-relations. A replay of the conference call will be available on the company's website for a period of 12 months and will be available shortly after the call.

Background Information

IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA), Boise, Idaho-based and formed in 1998, is a holding company comprised of Idaho Power, a regulated electric utility; IDACORP Financial, an investor in affordable housing and other real estate tax credit investments; and Ida-West Energy, an operator of small hydroelectric generation projects that satisfy the requirements of the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978. Idaho Power, headquartered in vibrant and fast-growing Boise, Idaho, has been a locally operated energy company since 1916. Today, it serves a 24,000-square-mile service area in Idaho and Oregon. With 17 low-cost hydropower projects at the core of its diverse energy mix, Idaho Power's residential, business, and agricultural customers pay among the nation's lowest prices for electricity. Its 2,100 employees proudly serve more than 660,000 customers with a culture of safety first, integrity always, and respect for all.

Press Release article 4 of 5, Source: WEC, 75 words

January 19: IDACORP quarterly dividends steady

IDACORP today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.0c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 88.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, February 05, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, February 05, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, March 02. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended January 19 were $3.46.

Press Release article 5 of 5, 304 words

December 31 2025: IDACORP: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

In this MD&A section of this report, the general financial condition and results of operations for IDACORP and its subsidiaries and Idaho Power and its subsidiary are discussed. The discussion of IDACORP's and Idaho Power's general financial condition and results of operations for 2024 compared with 2023 can be found in their Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024. See Part II - Item 7 - MD&A in that report for further information on the companies' prior period results of operations. While reading this MD&A, please refer to the accompanying consolidated financial statements of IDACORP and Idaho Power. Also refer to "Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements" and Part I - Item 1A - "Risk Factors" in this report for important information regarding forward-looking statements made in this MD&A section and elsewhere in this report.

INTRODUCTION

IDACORP is a holding company whose principal operating subsidiary is Idaho Power. IDACORP's common stock is listed and trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the trading symbol "IDA". Idaho Power is an electric utility whose rates and other matters are regulated by the IPUC, OPUC, and FERC. Idaho Power generates revenues and cash flows primarily from the sale and distribution of electricity to customers in its Idaho and Oregon service areas, as well as from the wholesale sale and transmission of electricity. On February 13, 2026, Idaho Power entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Oregon electric distribution business and associated distribution assets, as well as certain Oregon transmission assets, to OTEC. The closing of the transaction is subject to various conditions, including approvals of the OPUC, IPUC, and FERC.
